finitePositivePullbackStatus.label: finite positive augmented pullbacks
finitePositivePullbackStatus.reason: For each finite theta truncation, K_{E,N} is represented by a KLM pullback plus the augmented nonnegative trace repair.

compactTraceConvergenceStatus.label: compact-open and trace convergence
compactTraceConvergenceStatus.reason: K_{E,N}->K_E entrywise on finite compact evaluation sets and R_aug,N->R_aug in the transported graph-dual topology.

nonnegativeRepairStatus.label: nonnegative continuum repair
nonnegativeRepairStatus.reason: The narrow repair-consequence theorem exports D_aug>=0 on X_aug and the closed repaired-form conclusion, without importing the transport proof machinery into this closed-cone layer.

finitePsdConeClosureStatus.label: finite PSD cone closed
finitePsdConeClosureStatus.reason: Since c^*G_n c>=0 for every n and every c, the limit satisfies c^*G c>=0 for every c.  Hence G is positive semidefinite.

closedPositiveConeStatus.label: closed positive-cone limit
closedPositiveConeStatus.reason: The finite PSD cone closure theorem applies to each finite evaluation Gram matrix.  Since K_{E,N}->K_E entrywise and the finite augmented pullbacks are positive after repair, the full kernel K_E lies in the closed augmented KLM positive cone.

proof: Apply the finite augmented pullback theorem to each Xi_N.
Use compact-open convergence to pass K_{E,N} finite Gram entries to K_E.
Use the narrow continuum repair consequence to keep the augmented repair nonnegative.
Use the finite PSD cone closure theorem under entrywise limits.
